ShareCreate.description:
Request body for creating a read-sharing grant.

Target exactly one of: a member (`granted_member_id`), an identity
(`granted_identity_key`), or the whole project (omit both). Supplying both
a member and an identity target is rejected.

granted_identity_key.description: Target one asserted identity by its coalesced owner key (e.g. 'user:abc'). Mutually exclusive with `granted_member_id`; omit both for a project-wide grant.
granted_identity_key.title: Granted Identity Key

granted_member_id.description: Target one member; omit for a project-wide (or identity) grant
granted_member_id.title: Granted Member Id

agent.description: Runtime info for tasks that expose an interactive agent session

automation_id.title: Automation Id

identity_key.description: Coalesced caller identity that owns this task
identity_key.title: Identity Key

kind.description: Execution shape: agent conversation or one-shot process

runtime_id.description: CP-side Runtime this task is bound to (weak FK). Stamped on telemetry for per-Runtime analytics.
runtime_id.title: Runtime Id

TaskCancelMode.description:
How a task run cancel behaves.

``abort`` interrupts the in-flight turn immediately and keeps the sandbox
warm, so the task drops back to ``idle`` and the same container is resumable
on the next prompt — what an interactive "stop" button wants. ``drain`` lets
the active turn finish and return control to the user, then tears the sandbox
down at the next settle boundary (the task lands terminal/``cancelled`` and
the next prompt cold-boots on the current base image) — the release path.
See docs/design/sandbox-drain-on-release.md.
TaskCancelMode.title: TaskCancelMode

TaskCancelRequest.description:
Options for cancelling a task run.

Default (and the bodyless call) **aborts**: the in-flight turn is interrupted
immediately and the sandbox stays warm, so the task returns to ``idle`` and
the same container is resumable. Pass ``mode=drain`` to instead let the active
turn finish and tear the sandbox down at the next settle boundary (the task
lands ``cancelled`` and the next prompt cold-boots on the current base image);
a drained ``awaiting_user`` task keeps its ``metadata.pending_interrupts`` so
the HITL resume path rehydrates the question on the fresh sandbox.
See docs/design/sandbox-drain-on-release.md.

drain_within_seconds.description: Drain only: force teardown if the task has not reached a settle boundary within this many seconds. Omit to wait, bounded by the deployment drain ceiling.
drain_within_seconds.title: Drain Within Seconds

mode.description: Cancel behaviour: 'abort' (default) interrupts the in-flight turn now and keeps the sandbox warm/resumable; 'drain' lets the turn finish then tears the sandbox down at the next settle boundary (task -> cancelled). The bodyless call aborts.

TaskCancelRequest.title: TaskCancelRequest

TaskCancelResponse.description: Response returned after cancelling a task run.
TaskCancelResponse.title: TaskCancelResponse

agent_name.description: Recipe agent to run; omitted -> the recipe default (agents/agent.yaml)
agent_name.title: Agent Name

environment.description: Runtime environment for browser-created runtime tasks

fork_share_id.description: Fork from a shared conversation: the /v1/shares grant id for the source conversation. Persisted into metadata.forked_response and used by the runtime as the read capability when hydrating the forked history — it is itself the permissions boundary, so no share lookup happens at create. The fork seeds the new task with the whole shared conversation.
fork_share_id.title: Fork Share Id

idle_timeout_seconds.description: Override the interactive idle window (seconds) before the sandbox is torn down. 0 tears down as soon as it's provisioned (e.g. an empty-prompt warm/bake run); omit to use the deployment default. Clamped to the task timeout.
idle_timeout_seconds.title: Idle Timeout Seconds

runtime_group_id.description: Optional runtime group slug or id context for the task
runtime_group_id.title: Runtime Group Id

runtime_id.description: Runtime to resolve for this task (browser flow)
runtime_id.title: Runtime Id

TaskCreateResponse.description: Response returned after creating a task and its initial run.
TaskCreateResponse.title: TaskCreateResponse

TaskInclude.description:
Opt-in, non-default enrichments for a task read (`GET /v1/tasks/{id}?include=`).

The default task read is the cheapest path: a single-row Postgres lookup with
no enrichment. Each value here names an extra projection that costs more than
that bare read (an extra round-trip or join) and so is only computed when the
caller explicitly asks for it. Repeatable: `?include=agent&include=...`.

- ``agent`` — live sandbox status for an interactive, in-flight task, read
  from the Restate task workflow (one extra round-trip).
TaskInclude.title: TaskInclude

TaskKind.description:
Discriminates the two task execution shapes.

``agent`` boots the TS runtime-worker and runs an interactive LLM agent.
``process`` boots a one-shot Python script in the same image (a second
boot mode) and reports its result through the existing RESULT event /
completion-promise path. See docs/design/process-tasks.md.
TaskKind.title: TaskKind

TaskPrompt.description: Cursor-style prompt payload for a task run.
TaskPrompt.title: TaskPrompt

TaskRun.description: Run-shaped view over the currently active task execution.

id.description: Run identifier. Use 'current' until task runs are persisted separately.
id.title: Id

TaskRun.title: TaskRun

TaskRunCreate.description: Schema for creating a new run on an interactive task.

kind.description: Optional caller intent. 'prompt' requests a fresh turn; 'steer' injects into the active turn and falls back to prompt if no turn is active; 'clear' starts a new conversation/session in the same task sandbox.
kind.title: Kind

metadata.description: Metadata patch merged into the task before dispatching the run.
metadata.title: Metadata

resume.description: AG-UI resume entries answering the task's pending interrupt.
resume.title: Resume

TaskRunResponse.description: Response returned after creating a task run.
TaskRunResponse.title: TaskRunResponse

get.description:
Return org_id and project_id for the authenticated token.

Used by the frontend OTLP proxy to discover project context so it can
inject introspection.org.id / introspection.project.id into OTEL events
before forwarding to the processor.

schema.title: Response Get Me V1 Api Keys Me Get

get.description:
Verify an API key is valid.

Returns 200 OK if the key exists and is valid.
Returns 401 Unauthorized if the key is invalid or not found.

When the request passes through gateway ext_authz, Authorization is replaced
with the resolved DP JWT. The original API key is preserved in
X-Forwarded-Authorization, and this endpoint uses that value so it still
verifies the raw API key registration path end to end.

Also publishes an `api_key.ping` event to Redis pub/sub for WebSocket
subscribers to verify the connection is working.

Usage:
    curl -H "Authorization: <intro-api-key>" http://dp/v1/api-keys/ping

This is intentionally minimal - no claims or details returned
to prevent information disclosure.

parameters.description: Original Authorization header preserved by gateway ext_authz
parameters.name: x-forwarded-authorization

schema.description: Original Authorization header preserved by gateway ext_authz
schema.title: X-Forwarded-Authorization

get.description:
List GenAI conversations with filtering and pagination.

Requires scope: `conversations:read`, `read`, or `*`

Returns aggregated conversation summaries from ClickHouse traces.
A conversation may contain multiple traces linked by conversation ID, and
the summary aggregates metrics across those traces.

**Ownership:**
Non-privileged callers see only their own conversations (owner-key scoped,
same predicate as item/detail reads). Valid `share_id` capabilities widen
the page with the conversations those shares grant.

**Pagination:**
Use the `next` token from the response to fetch subsequent pages.
The token encodes the cursor position and must be passed unchanged.

**Filtering:**
All filters are optional and combined with AND logic.
Date range filters are inclusive.

parameters.description: Include conversations granted by these /v1/shares capabilities (one or many)

schema.title: Share Id

Postgres reads/writes go through short-lived sessions so no pooled connection
is held across the Control Plane issue-sync cleanup call.

Requires scope: `issues:read`, `read`, or `*`

Requires scope: `issues:write`, `write`, or `*`

Postgres reads/writes go through short-lived sessions so no pooled connection
is held across the Control Plane issue-sync cleanup call.

post.description: Execute one bounded metrics query for the authenticated project.

Exchange a CP token for a DP session.

This is the programmatic flow for session creation (vs OAuth redirect flow).
Used by the frontend when it already has a CP-authenticated browser session and just needs
to establish a DP session.

Flow:
1. Frontend has an authenticated CP session and calls CP `/v1/tokens`
2. CP validates that auth context and returns access + refresh tokens
3. Frontend calls this endpoint with the access token
4. DP validates JWT, creates session, sets cookie

Security:
- JWT is validated against CP's signing key
- JWT is short-lived (5 min) to prevent replay
- Session is bound to a new session_id (prevents session fixation)

get.description:
Get current session information (read-only, no auto-refresh).

This endpoint does NOT refresh the session - it only reports current state.
Sessions are refreshed automatically by actual API calls via get_auth_context.
Frontend should handle 401 from API calls to trigger re-auth.

post.description:
Refresh a session using the refresh token cookie.

This endpoint:
1. Validates the refresh token against Redis
2. Rotates the refresh token (generates new one, invalidates old)
3. Returns new session and refresh cookies
4. Returns {"success": true} on success, {"success": false, "error": "..."} on failure

Security:
- Refresh tokens are single-use (rotated on each refresh)
- Old tokens are immediately invalidated to prevent replay attacks

get.description:
List reports for the authenticated project.

Full markdown content is intentionally omitted from list responses.
Use `GET /v1/reports/{report_id}` to fetch a full report.

Requires scope: `reports:read`, `read`, or `*`

Use the `next` token from the response to fetch subsequent pages.

Read a single sharing grant.

Returns the grant with a fully-qualified `url` to the shared resource. With
`?redirect=true`, responds `302` to that resource instead. Scope: `shares:read`.

Rows the caller doesn't own (identity tier, else member tier) are excluded.
Use the `next` token from the response to fetch subsequent pages.

post.description:
Create a new task.

Runtime + identity resolution is now JWT-claim-driven (see
`docs/design/sdk-api.md`). When the caller authenticates with a
CP-minted **runner JWT** (`token_type == "runner"`), the claims
carry the resolved `runtime_id`, optional `experiment_id`, and
`identity` — DP looks up the recipe pin via the resolver and
stamps everything onto the task. When the caller authenticates
with a non-runner credential (internal service tokens, agent
sessions), no runtime is selected and the task runs against the
project's default agent path.

Provisions a sandbox. The sandbox reads INTROSPECTION_AGENT_NAME from its
environment to select the managed Pi agent definition.

Requires scope: `tasks:write`, `write`, or `*`

delete.description:
Soft delete a task.

The task must be archived first. Archive the task before deleting.

Requires scope: ``tasks:delete``, ``delete``, or ``*``

get.description:
Get a task by ID.

By default this is the cheapest read available: a single-row lookup of the
task projection from Postgres, with **no** enrichment and **no** Restate
round-trip. The ``status`` field is maintained on the row by the task
workflow, so this is the quick way to check a task's status (e.g. a readiness
poll or catch-up read) without loading Restate.

Anything that costs more than that bare read is opt-in via ``?include=`` (a
repeatable query param, see ``TaskInclude``). ``?include=agent`` enriches an
interactive, in-flight task with live sandbox status from the Restate
workflow — one extra round-trip — so the interactive task-detail view opts in
while status checks do not. Live, ongoing output belongs on ``/stream`` (SSE);
this endpoint never streams.

Requires scope: `tasks:read`, `read`, or `*`

parameters.description: Opt-in non-default enrichments (e.g. `agent` for live sandbox status); repeatable
parameters.name: include

schema.description: Opt-in non-default enrichments (e.g. `agent` for live sandbox status); repeatable
schema.title: Include

post.description:
Archive a task, cancelling any active work in the background.

Always sets ``is_archived = true``. If the task is still active,
also cancels the Restate workflow so the sandbox is cleaned up.

Requires scope: ``tasks:write``, ``write``, or ``*``

Create a run on an interactive task.

Running tasks receive steering guidance. Idle/pending tasks receive a prompt.
Completed, failed, or unavailable sandboxes are restarted implicitly.
Clear keeps the current task sandbox alive and replaces only the
in-sandbox conversation/session. Attached local recipes are synced by the
runtime recipe watcher before clear/restart.
This is the existing interactive-task subresource surface; new resources
should still use CRUD-shaped routes.

get.description: Get status for a task run on the existing interactive-task subresource.

post.description:
Cancel the current run for an active interactive task.

Default (including the bodyless call) **aborts**: the in-flight turn is
interrupted immediately and the sandbox stays warm, so the task returns to
``idle`` and the same container is resumable on the next prompt. Pass
``mode=drain`` to instead let the active turn finish and tear the sandbox
down at the next settle boundary so the next prompt cold-boots on the
current base image — the release/rollover path
(docs/design/sandbox-drain-on-release.md).

get.description:
Stream AG-UI output for one task run.

``wait_for_start`` **defaults to ``true``** — the legacy long-poll: the
server holds the stream open, emitting ``queued``/``starting`` lifecycle
frames, until the run is live. This default exists for backward
compatibility with clients that do not send the param (notably the SDKs,
which call the bare endpoint). Pass ``wait_for_start=0`` to opt into the
target "not ready, retry" contract: a pre-hardware run first holds up to
the advertised ``Retry-After`` window; only if it remains not attachable
does it return ``429`` + ``Retry-After`` + a ``{"status": <phase>}`` body.
Our own clients (frontend, capacity harness) send ``0``; the default flips
to ``0`` (or the param is removed) once every client — including the
external SDKs — has migrated.

The reconnect resume cursor — the last content-frame seq the client
received (the SSE ``id:`` of worker content frames) — arrives either as
``since`` (explicit query form, our frontend) or as the SSE-standard
``Last-Event-ID`` header (the SDKs' ``streamResumable``, see
``docs/design/sdk-resumable-streams.md``). It is forwarded to the
runtime-worker so it replays the frames the client missed while
disconnected (or emits a ``CUSTOM resume_gap`` frame when the disconnect
outlived its bounded replay buffer). Non-numeric header values
(connection-local ``c-…`` control-frame ids) are not cursors and are
ignored.

post.description: Unarchive a task so it can accept new runs.
